across.clue: To consider a problem or decision carefully. | ___ over
To continue to develop or move forward.
A class at a college or university in which a small group of students discusses a subject with a teacher.
Happening or existing for a long time or for all time in the future.
a situation in which separate things happen by chance at the same time or in the same way.
Someone who has a degree from a university.
To decide that something will not be done at the time when it was planned for but at a later time.
an organisation to which you give money so that it can give money and help to people who are poor or ill or who need advice and support
important and directly connected to what is being discussed or considered.

down.clue: a public occasion when things are sold to the people who offer the most money for them
someone who walks for long distances in the countryside for pleasure.
a man whose job is to look after the passengers on a plane train or ship especially serving them with food and drink.
An approximate calculation or judgment of the size amount or cost of something.
a legal agreement in which you borrow money from a bank in order to buy a house.
Someone or something that is efficient works well and produces good results by using the available time money supplies etc in the most effective way.
someone who is walking especially in a town or city instead of driving or riding.
Relating to education especially education in colleges and universities.

an arrangement in which you regularly pay an insurance company or other organisation an amount of money so that they will give you money if something you own is damaged lost or stolen or if you die or are ill or injured.
to say very firmly that something must happen or be done.
To continue to be in a particular situation or condition.
